2603.28537.title: Training data generation for context-dependent rubric-based short answer grading
2603.28537.paper_summary: Every 4 years, the PISA test is administered by the OECD to test the knowledge of teenage students worldwide and allow for comparisons of educational systems. However, having to avoid language differences and annotator bias makes the grading of student answers challenging. For these reasons, it would be interesting to compare methods of automatic student answer grading. To train some of these methods, which require machine learning, or to compute parameters or select hyperparameters for those that do not, a large amount of domain-specific data is needed. In this work, we explore a small number of methods for creating a large-scale training dataset using only a relatively small confidential dataset as a reference, leveraging a set of very simple derived text formats to preserve confidentiality. Using these methods, we successfully created three surrogate datasets that are, at the very least, superficially more similar to the reference dataset than purely the result of prompt-based generation. Early experiments suggest one of these approaches might also lead to improved model training.
2603.28537.paper_summary_zh: 每四年，OECD 會進行 PISA 測試，以測試全球青少年學生的知識並允許教育系統之間的比較。<lb/>然而，必須避免語言差異和標註者偏見使得學生答案的評分變得具有挑戰性。<lb/>基於這些原因，對於自動學生答案評分方法的比較將是有趣的。<lb/>為了訓練這些需要機器學習的方法，或計算參數或選擇不需要機器學習的方法的超參數，需要大量特定領域的數據。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們探索了一小部分方法，僅使用相對較小的機密數據集作為參考，創建大型訓練數據集，利用一組非常簡單的衍生文本格式來保護機密性。<lb/>使用這些方法，我們成功創建了三個替代數據集，這些數據集至少在表面上比純粹基於提示生成的結果更類似於參考數據集。<lb/>早期實驗表明，這些方法中的一種可能也會導致模型訓練的改進。
2603.28537.author: Pavel Šindelář et.al.
2603.28537.authors: Pavel Šindelář, Dávid Slivka, Christopher Bouma, Filip Prášil, Ondřej Bojar
